A restante bibliografia dependerá do tema do projecto a realizar por cada aluno ou grupo
methodology: Personalized tutoring; real-world scenário development; professional grade work
objectives:
The main purpose of this course is to integrate the concepts that were acquired by the student throughout the course, a project of broad scope and where students autonomously solve a problem.

This general objective can be achieved by:

LO1. Know how to apply the principles and techniques associated with the design and implementation of real or simulated cases.

LO2. Knowing how to apply the knowledge obtained in the course units in the face of problems related to them, as well as the application of knowledge to new IS / ICT paradigms not covered in the course.

LO3. Know how to investigate the state of the art in the object of study.

LO4. Knowing how to develop work of an applied nature that integrates the knowledge acquired throughout the curricular component of the course.
organicUnit: ECATI - School of Communication, Architecture, Arts and Information Technologies
programme:
S1. Guidance.

S2. Development of the state of the art.

S3. Project development.

      S3.1. Inception

      S3.2. Project Planning

      S3.3. Feasibility Analysis

      S3.4. Execution

      S3.5. Project Monitoring

      S3.6. Tests

S4. Results presentation.
presentation:
The Final Course Work is a fundamental element of the curriculum of the undergraduate courses, working as a core moment in the training of students, namely in order to promote

their professional integration and/or initiation to scientific work.

The Final Course Work should correspond to a applied project in the area under study, which may include the development of projects for relevant solutions within course contents, software development or more theoretical or academic projects. The TFC should lead to final results that can be applied in real context (apparatus, program, method, conceptual model, contribution to research or conclusions about a problem).
